name: findpython

metadata.name: findpython
metadata.summary: A utility to find python versions on your system

documentation:
# FindPython _A utility to find python versions on your system._ [![Tests](https://github.com/frostming/findpython/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/frostming/findpython/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/findpython?logo=python&logoColor=%23cccccc&style=flat-square)](https://pypi.org/project/findpython) [![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/findpython?logo=python&logoColor=%23cccccc&style=flat-square)](https://pypi.org/project/findpython) [![pdm-managed](https://img.shields.io/badge/pdm-managed-blueviolet?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/frostming/findpython) ## Description This library is a rewrite of [pythonfinder] project by [@techalchemy][techalchemy]. It simplifies the whole code structure while preserving most of the original features. [pythonfinder]: https://github.com/sarugaku/pythonfinder [techalchemy]: https://github.com/techalchemy ## Installation FindPython is installable via any kind of package manager including `pip`: ```bash pip install findpython ```
Expand this section to see findpython's availability in the package ecosystem
## Usage ```python >>> import findpython >>> findpython.find(3, 9) # Find by major and minor version , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=10> >>> findpython.find("3.9") # Find by version string , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=10> >>> findpython.find("3.9-32") # Find by version string and architecture , architecture='32bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=10> >>> findpython.find(name="python3") # Find by executable name , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=10, patch=2> >>> findpython.find("python3") # Find by executable name without keyword argument, same as above , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=10, patch=2> >>> findpython.find_all(major=3, minor=9) # Same arguments as `find()`, but return all matches [, architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=10>, , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=10>, , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=9>, , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=5>, , architecture='64bit', major=3, minor=9, patch=5>] ``` ## CLI Usage In addition, FindPython provides a CLI interface to find python versions: ``` usage: findpython [-h] [-V] [-a] [--resolve-symlink] [-v] [--no-same-file] [--no-same-python] [--providers PROVIDERS] [version_spec] A utility to find python versions on your system positional arguments: version_spec Python version spec or name options: -h, --help show this help message and exit -V, --version show program's version number and exit -a, --all Show all matching python versions --resolve-symlink Resolve all symlinks -v, --verbose Verbose output --no-same-file Eliminate the duplicated results with the same file contents --no-same-python Eliminate the duplicated results with the same sys.executable --providers PROVIDERS Select provider(s) to use ``` ## Integration FindPython finds Python from the following places: - `PATH` environment variable - pyenv install root - asdf python install root - [rye](https://rye-up.com) toolchain install root - `/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions` (MacOS) - Windows registry (Windows only) ## License FindPython is released under MIT License.

functions.name: findpython.utils.safe_iter_dir
documentation:
Iterate over a directory, returning an empty iterator if the path
is not a directory or is not readable.

functions.name: findpython.utils.path_is_known_executable
documentation:
Returns whether a given path is a known executable from known executable extensions
or has the executable bit toggled.

```
param path

The path to the target executable.

type path



return

True if the path has chmod +x, or is a readable, known executable extension.

rtype

bool
```

functions.name: findpython.utils.looks_like_python
documentation:
Determine whether the supplied filename looks like a possible name of python.

```
param str name

The name of the provided file.

return

Whether the provided name looks like python.

rtype

bool
```

functions.name: findpython.utils.path_is_python
documentation:
Determine whether the supplied path is a executable and looks like
a possible path to python.

```
param path

The path to an executable.

type path



return

Whether the provided path is an executable path to python.

rtype

bool
```

functions.name: findpython.utils.get_binary_hash
documentation: Return the MD5 hash of the given file.

documentation: Parse the version dict from the version string

documentation: Run a script and return the output.

methods.name: findpython.python.PythonVersion.matches
documentation:
Return True if the python matches the provided criteria.

```
param major

The major version to match.

type major

int

param minor

The minor version to match.

type minor

int

param patch

The micro version to match.

type patch

int

param pre

Whether the python is a prerelease.

type pre

bool

param dev

Whether the python is a devrelease.

type dev

bool

param name

The name of the python.

type name

str

param architecture

The architecture of the python.

type architecture

str

param implementation

The implementation of the python.

type implementation

str

return

Whether the python matches the provided criteria.

rtype

bool
```

documentation: Get the version of the python.

documentation: Sort by the version, then by length of the executable path.
The single Python version object found by pythonfinder.

modules.name: findpython
documentation:
FindPython
~~~~~~~~~~
A utility to find python versions on your system

functions.name: findpython.find
documentation:
Return the Python version that is closest to the given version criteria.

```
param major

The major version or the version string or the name to match.

param minor

The minor version to match.

param patch

The micro version to match.

param pre

Whether the python is a prerelease.

param dev

Whether the python is a devrelease.

param name

The name of the python.

param architecture

The architecture of the python.

return

a Python object or None
```

functions.name: findpython.find_all
documentation:
Return all Python versions matching the given version criteria.

```
param major

The major version or the version string or the name to match.

param minor

The minor version to match.

param patch

The micro version to match.

param pre

Whether the python is a prerelease.

param dev

Whether the python is a devrelease.

param name

The name of the python.

param architecture

The architecture of the python.

return

a list of PythonVersion objects
```

functions.name: findpython.register_provider
documentation:
Register a provider to use when finding python versions.

```
param provider

A provider class
```

classes.name: findpython.finder.Finder

methods.name: findpython.finder.Finder.add_provider
documentation:
Add provider to the provider list.
If pos is given, it will be inserted at the given position.

methods.name: findpython.finder.Finder.find_all
documentation:
Return all Python versions matching the given version criteria.

```
param major

The major version or the version string or the name to match.

param minor

The minor version to match.

param patch

The micro version to match.

param pre

Whether the python is a prerelease.

param dev

Whether the python is a devrelease.

param name

The name of the python.

param architecture

The architecture of the python.

param allow_prereleases

Whether to allow prereleases.

param implementation

The implementation of the python. E.g. "cpython", "pypy".

return

a list of PythonVersion objects
```

methods.name: findpython.finder.Finder.find

```
param major

The major version or the version string or the name to match.

param minor

The minor version to match.

param patch

The micro version to match.

param pre

Whether the python is a prerelease.

param dev

param name

The name of the python.

param architecture

param allow_prereleases
```

methods.name: findpython.finder.Finder._find_all_python_versions
documentation: Find all python versions on the system.

Find python versions on the system.

```
param resolve_symlinks

Whether to resolve symlinks.

param no_same_file

Whether to deduplicate with the python executable content.

param no_same_interpreter

Whether to deduplicate with the python executable path.
```

methods.name: findpython.providers.base.BaseProvider.find_pythons_from_path
documentation:
A general helper method to return pythons under a given path.

```
param path

The path to search for pythons

param as_interpreter

Use the path as the interpreter path.
If the pythons might be a wrapper script, don't set this to True.

returns

An iterable of PythonVersion objects
```
The base class for python providers
